parameters.subject: =Your Airfit Program is Ready!|{{ $('Workout Recommendation Agent').item.json.output.title }}

options.systemMessage:
You are an HTML renderer. Output a single, complete HTML document that matches the provided template and fills it with the provided content. Hard rules: - Output ONLY raw HTML. No markdown. No backticks. No explanations. - Use EXACTLY the HTML structure and CSS from the template below. Do not add or remove CSS rules. Do not add external assets. - Replace template placeholders with the provided content. Do not invent text or add extra sections beyond what the template defines. - Convert newline characters in overview, closing, and all inserted text blocks into
tags. - Convert markdown bold markers **like this** into like this. Do not implement other markdown features. - Escape HTML special characters in the content (& < > " ') before inserting, except for the
and tags you create. - The number of phase cards must equal the number of phases in the input array, in the same order. - Each phase card must include: name, dates, objective, weekly structure (sessions_per_week + items + optional_add_ons), progression_focus, risk_to_manage, success_signals. - weekly_structure.items, progression_focus, risk_to_manage, success_signals are arrays. Render them as bullet lines with exact format: • {item}
No leading spaces. No blank line before the first bullet. Do not use
    /
  • . - Render assumptions as 3 labeled lines in this exact order (do NOT output raw keys like session_length:): Session length: {assumptions.session_length}

    Winter constraint: {assumptions.winter_constraint}

    Strength approach: {assumptions.strength_approach} - If a field is missing, render it as an empty string (do not omit sections). Template (do not change): Program Email

    {{TITLE}}

    Dates: {{DATES}}

    Overview

    {{OVERVIEW_HTML}}

    Assumptions

    {{ASSUMPTIONS_HTML}}

    Phases

    {{PHASE_CARDS}}

    Closing

    {{CLOSING_HTML}}
    — Your coach
    Phase card template (repeat for each phase, do not change):

    {{PHASE_NAME}}

    Dates: {{PHASE_DATES}}
    Objective: {{PHASE_OBJECTIVE_HTML}}

    Weekly structure ({{PHASE_SESSIONS_PER_WEEK}} sessions/week):
    {{PHASE_WEEKLY_ITEMS_HTML}}
    Optional add-ons: {{PHASE_OPTIONAL_ADD_ONS_HTML}}

    Progression focus:
    {{PHASE_PROGRESSION_FOCUS_HTML}}

    Risks to manage:
    {{PHASE_RISK_TO_MANAGE_HTML}}

    Success signals:
    {{PHASE_SUCCESS_SIGNALS_HTML}}

body.user_goals: I want to drop bf from ca 15-6% to 11-2% and drop a couple of kgs, while keeping my overall physique.
body.trade_offs: Fine with deprioritizing muscle growth in favor of looking leaner.
body.user_context: Work from home, flexible schedule, gym in afternoons, Friday HIIT with friends.
body.other_preferences: No supersets. No gym on weekends.
body.injury_status: Slight discomfort on outside of left knee with high cardio volume.

output.title: Daniel’s 3-Month Lean-Down Performance Plan 🏁
output.overview:
Hey Daniel — welcome to the program 👋

You’ve got a strong training background, a flexible work-from-home setup, a full gym, and a standing Friday HIIT date with friends, which is honestly a pretty efficient way to make suffering social. Based on your goal order — leanness first, endurance second, strength third — I’ve mapped your 3-month plan into 3 phases that keep your physique intact while pushing body-fat reduction with enough cardio to matter and enough strength work to avoid looking like you accidentally misplaced your muscle.

I also used your recent history to shape this: you’re already training across strength, cycling, HIIT, running, and climbing, so this won’t be a random personality transplant. That said, your left knee discomfort with higher cardio volume means we’ll bias lower-impact conditioning a bit more than your ambition might prefer. Safety wins; ego can sit quietly in the corner.

Because your start date is just ahead, this phase map is set up in advance. Then, each Sunday evening, the weekly plan can plug into this structure.

assumptions.session_length: Most sessions are planned around 45–75 minutes, with one longer endurance session when useful. Occasional low-risk double days are acceptable because you’re open to them and your schedule is flexible.
assumptions.winter_constraint: You’re in Munich, and although the program starts at the end of March, early-phase conditioning may still lean on indoor cycling/treadmill or controlled outdoor sessions depending on weather and footing. No heroic icy run decisions required.
assumptions.strength_approach: Strength training will prioritize maintenance of muscle and overall physique rather than aggressive hypertrophy, using straightforward sets with no supersets and mostly 2–3 gym sessions per week on weekdays only.

phases.name: 🌱 Phase 1 — Foundation & Knee-Smart Ramp-Up
phases.objective: Re-establish consistent 5-day training rhythm, build calorie-burning volume through controlled conditioning, and lock in strength maintenance without spiking knee irritation.

items: 2 weekday strength sessions focused on big lifts and physique maintenance
1 low-impact endurance session, preferably cycling or indoor bike
1 controlled run or run-alternative aerobic session kept moderate in volume
1 Friday HIIT session with friends, managed so it stays hard but not reckless
Optional easy bouldering/mobility style session only if recovery is clearly good, not as a sixth hard day
weekly_structure.optional_add_ons: Short walks or easy recovery spins can be used between sessions, but weekends stay out of the gym as requested.

progression_focus: Keep strength numbers relatively stable while trimming overall fatigue
Progress aerobic volume mainly through cycling and controlled easy-moderate efforts
Cap running volume increases conservatively to protect the left knee
risk_to_manage: Left knee discomfort from too much running or stacked high-impact cardio
Going too hard too early because your engine says yes before your joints agree
Letting HIIT plus running create hidden fatigue that leaks into strength work
success_signals: You complete 5 sessions most weeks without needing a bailout workout shuffle
Knee discomfort stays mild or improves
Strength performance is stable on key lifts
Cardio feels more repeatable, not just survivable

phases.name: 🔥 Phase 2 — Leaning Out & Work Capacity Build
phases.objective: Drive the main body-composition push by increasing weekly energy expenditure and work capacity while preserving muscle and keeping endurance progression joint-friendly.

items: 2 strength sessions with slightly denser total work but still no supersets
1 quality endurance session centered on cycling intervals or tempo work
1 aerobic support session that alternates between easy run, bike, or mixed conditioning depending on knee status
1 Friday HIIT session with friends as the main weekly high-intensity anchor
Bouldering can occasionally replace the aerobic support session when recovery or motivation needs a refresh
weekly_structure.optional_add_ons: If recovery is solid, one short same-day easy spin or walk can pair with a strength day to increase expenditure without adding much impact.

progression_focus: Increase conditioning quality without turning every session into a death march
Use low-impact cardio to carry more total work
Maintain strength intensity enough to preserve physique
Refine exercise selection and weekly flow so hard days stop stepping on each other
risk_to_manage: Accumulated fatigue from intense preference plus social HIIT plus extra cardio
Knee flare-ups if running creeps up too fast
Dropping strength quality by chasing calorie burn in every session
success_signals: You feel fitter across HIIT and endurance sessions, not just lighter
Bodyweight trends modestly downward while gym performance remains mostly intact
You’re finishing sessions with something left in the tank instead of seeing your soul leave your body

phases.name: ⚡ Phase 3 — Consolidate, Sharpen & Exit Lean
phases.objective: Hold the leaner look, keep performance sharp, and reduce unnecessary fatigue so you finish the block looking and performing better rather than crawling over the line.

items: 2 strength sessions focused on maintaining load and movement quality with slightly lower total volume
1 quality cardio session, mostly bike-based or mixed modal to limit impact
1 easier aerobic session for recovery support and energy expenditure
1 Friday HIIT session that stays punchy but slightly better controlled than in mid-block
Optional light outdoor ride or bouldering when weather and recovery line up, without turning weekends into a bonus training camp
weekly_structure.optional_add_ons: Extra activity stays easy and restorative only; this phase is about arriving fresh, not collecting tiredness like a hobby.

progression_focus: Maintain results with cleaner fatigue management
Keep intensity touchpoints while trimming junk volume
Protect joints and energy levels as cumulative load peaks
Finish with sustainable momentum rather than burnout
risk_to_manage: Trying to squeeze in extra work because the finish line is close
Knee irritation from late-block running enthusiasm
Carrying too much fatigue into the final weeks and flattening performance
success_signals: You feel sharp rather than stale
Conditioning sessions stay strong with less mental drag
You end the 3 months leaner, lighter, and structurally intact — always a nice touch

output.closing:
That’s the 3-phase map, Daniel 📬

From here, the weekly plans can slot neatly into this structure every Sunday evening: enough intensity to match your preference, enough restraint to respect your knee, and enough strength work to keep your physique from filing a formal complaint. Solid base, hard middle, cleaner finish — simple, effective, and pleasantly free of weekend gym captivity.
